purpose.summary: This COBOL program interacts with an IMS database to retrieve and update pending authorization details. It retrieves authorization summary and detail segments based on account ID and authorization key, and allows for marking authorization records as fraudulent.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports a fraud detection or authorization management process within a financial institution or similar organization.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing account ID and other summary information.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing authorization key and other detailed information, including fraud reporting date and removal flag.

inputs.name: WS-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID passed to the program, used to retrieve authorization records.

inputs.name: WS-AUTH-KEY
inputs.description: Authorization key passed to the program, used to retrieve authorization details.

inputs.name: WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD
inputs.description: Fraud authorization record passed to the program, used to update the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment.

outputs.name: PAUTDTL1
outputs.description: Updated Pending Authorization Details segment, with fraud indicators set.

outputs.name: CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK
outputs.description: Rolls back any database changes made during the transaction.

outputs.name: CICS SYNCPOINT
outputs.description: Commits any database changes made during the transaction.

business_rules.description: If the IMS return code indicates a segment is not found or the end of the database is reached, the program sets the AUTHS-EOF flag to TRUE.
If the IMS return code indicates an error other than segment not found or end of database, the program sets the WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message, and displays it on the AUTHVIEW screen.
If the fraud is removed, a message 'AUTH FRAUD REMOVED...' is moved to WS-MESSAGE. Otherwise, a message 'AUTH MARKED FRAUD...' is moved to WS-MESSAGE.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates PA-FRAUD-REMOVED after IMS REPL call.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves pending authorization summary and detail records from an IMS database. It first schedules the PSB using SCHEDULE-PSB. It then moves the account ID (WS-ACCT-ID) and authorization key (WS-AUTH-KEY) to the corresponding fields in the PAUTSUM0 segment. It performs a GU (Get Unique) operation to retrieve the PAUTSUM0 segment based on the account ID. If successful, it then performs a GNP (Get Next within Parent) operation to retrieve the PAUTDTL1 segment based on the authorization key. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code after each IMS call. If an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph sets AUTHS-EOF flag if segment is not found or end of database is reached.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database. It performs a GNP (Get Next within Parent) operation to retrieve the PAUTDTL1 segment. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code after the IMS call. If an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph sets AUTHS-EOF flag if segment is not found or end of database is reached.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization details record in the IMS database. It moves the fraud authorization record (WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD) to the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. It then performs a REPL (Replace) operation to update the PAUTDTL1 segment in the IMS database. After the update, it checks the DIBSTAT return code. If successful, it performs TAKE-SYNCPOINT to commit the changes and constructs a message indicating whether the fraud was marked or removed. If an error occurs, it performs ROLL-BACK to undo the changes, constructs an error message, and displays it using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT command to commit the changes made to the database. This ensures that all updates are permanently saved. It does not take any inputs or produce any specific outputs other than committing the transaction. It is called after a successful update of the authorization details.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK command to undo any changes made to the database. This is used to revert the database to its previous state in case of an error during the update process. It does not take any inputs or produce any specific outputs other than rolling back the transaction. It is called when an error occurs during the update of authorization details.

This paragraph schedules the PSB (Program Specification Block) for the IMS database. It first attempts to schedule the PSB using the SCHD command with the PSB-NAME. If the PSB has been scheduled more than once (indicated by PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE), it terminates the PSB using the TERM command and then schedules it again. Error handling is performed by checking the DIBSTAT return code. If an error occurs during scheduling, an error message is constructed and displayed using SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. If the scheduling is successful, the IMS-PSB-SCHD flag is set to TRUE.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB in READ-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message with the IMS return code, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB in READ-NEXT-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message with the IMS return code, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK in UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS
error_handling.action: Performs ROLL-BACK to undo changes, sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message with the IMS return code, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK in SCHEDULE-PSB
error_handling.action: Sets WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', constructs an error message with the IMS return code, and calls SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN to display the error.
The PSB is terminated and rescheduled.

cics_operations.purpose: Rolls back changes to the database.

open_questions.question: What is the purpose of SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN?
open_questions.context: The code calls this paragraph to display error messages, but the implementation is not provided.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the source code or documentation for the SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN paragraph to determine its functionality.

open_questions.question: What is the structure of WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD?
open_questions.context: The code moves this record to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS, but the fields within this record are unknown.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the copybook definition for WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD to determine its structure.
